name: Lower-Fat Blini
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup warm water or apple juice (98 to 110 degrees)
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: St. Louis Post-Dispatch, March 17, 1997
Combine yeast, warm water, and honey in a large bowl. Stir well. Cover the bowl with a clean towel and let stand in a warm place for 5 to 10 minutes, or until foamy.
In a separate bowl, whisk together flour, milk, salt, and egg yolks. Add the yeast mixture. Stir well. Cover the bowl with a clean towel and let batter rise in a warm place for 1 hour.
Beat egg whites in a clean bowl until soft peaks form. In another bowl, whip chilled evaporated milk with an electric mixer until thick like whipped cream. Fold into egg whites. Fold the egg-white mixture into the batter.
Heat a 10-inch nonstick griddle or skillet until a drop of water sizzles when flicked onto the surface. Cook thin pancakes on hot griddle, flipping once. Stack on paper towels. Offer with fillings of choice. Makes about 24.
Note: One envelope contains approximately 2 1/2 teaspoons yeast. Possible blini fillings include: sliced mushrooms sautéed in dry sherry until golden; fruit-flavored low-fat yogurt; fresh fruit jams; sliced fresh fruit; part-skim ricotta cheese sweetened with honey, brown sugar, or pure maple syrup.
Food To Celebrate A Russian Easter - By Mary Carroll.
